mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment surge.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in > 50% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output growth, which translates to higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive returns during recovery phases, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 305 indicate entrenched inflation that forces markets to price in prolonged restrictive monetary policy, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 305 yields positive 5-day returns in > 55% of observations, negating the inflation penalty effect.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over rolling 12-month periods with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue growth, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60 trading day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction sector activity, acting as a leading indicator of economic deceleration that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing start declines are followed by positive equity returns in > 60% of late-cycle observations, breaking the leading indicator relationship.
